content.problemStatement:
Chef had an array AAA of length NNN of **positive** integers. He calculated an array BBB of N‚àí1N - 1N‚àí1 integers, where Bi=Ai+Ai+1B_i = A_i + A_{i + 1}Bi‚Äã=Ai‚Äã+Ai+1‚Äã.

For example, if A=[2,5,4]A = [2, 5, 4]A=[2,5,4], he got B=[2+5,5+4]=[7,9]B = [2 + 5, 5 + 4] = [7, 9]B=[2+5,5+4]=[7,9].

Unfortunately, Chef has forgotten the array AAA, and the elements in his sequence BBB have been shuffled. Given the shuffled sequence BBB, predict the original array AAA. If multiple answers exist, all will be accepted. It is guaranteed that the input data is generated in such a way that a valid array AAA exists.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains NNN - the size of the array AAA.
The second line contains N‚àí1N - 1N‚àí1 integers - the shuffled sequence BBB.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line NNN integers - A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2, ..., A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,...,AN‚Äã - one of the possible original sequences, Ai>0A_i > 0Ai‚Äã>0 for all iii.


If multiple answers are possible, all will be accepted.
constraints: The input is generated such that there exists a valid array AAA
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : It is possible that the initial sequence A=[2,5,4]A = [2, 5, 4]A=[2,5,4] because then the constructed initial BBB would be [7,9][7, 9][7,9] and then it got shuffled to [9,7][9, 7][9,7]. A=[4,5,2]A = [4, 5, 2]A=[4,5,2] is also a valid initial sequence.
